title: 5-story apartment building could replace vacant Syracuse Walgreens
summary: A developer proposes a 246-unit, 5-story apartment building on Syracuse's East Side near Syracuse University, potentially converting a vacant Walgreens property. This residential development signals private sector investment in neighborhood revitalization and may impact local zoning, permitting, and economic development priorities.

political_signal.description: Development proposal aligns with city housing goals and neighborhood revitalization on East Side; demonstrates private market confidence in Syracuse residential market near institutional anchor (SU).

processing_notes: No specific developer name provided in excerpt; full article likely contains identity
No dollar amounts or timeline specified in provided content
Proximity to Syracuse University is significant context for market analysis
Vacant Walgreens represents land reuse/brownfield consideration—may trigger environmental or remediation review
